title: What's in Their Pockets? (Fantasy)
description: The mundane contents of a searched pocket, bag, or body — for loot, NPC color, or found-object flavor in a fantasy setting.

entries.result: Three copper coins, two of which are from a kingdom that no longer exists.
A folded piece of paper with a hand-drawn map to something. The map has no labels.
A small leather pouch of dried herbs. The smell is pleasant. The use is unclear.
A wax-sealed letter addressed to someone who is probably not this person.
Half a biscuit, wrapped in cloth.
A small wooden token carved with a guild mark.
A length of string with seven knots tied in it.
A single earring — the mate is missing.
A pressed flower, dried flat, wrapped in wax paper.
A list of names written in different inks, suggesting it was added to over time.
A die carved from bone, worn smooth from use.
A fishhook wrapped in leather to prevent it from catching on the fabric.
A small piece of chalk, half-used.
A folded broadsheet with one article torn out.
A lock of hair tied with red thread.
A receipt for an item not yet picked up from a craftsman's shop.
A small stoppered vial of oil. The cap is sticky.
A button that doesn't match any button visible on the owner's clothing.
A coin with a hole drilled through it and a worn cord looped through.
A piece of flint and a fire steel, wrapped together.
A short stub of candle.
Three dried peas.
A folded note that reads only: 'Not yet.'
A small comb with two broken teeth.
A tiny painted portrait of an unfamiliar face.
A single iron nail, very old.
A token from a specific gambling house, good for one drink.
A small roll of bandage, partially used.
A short pencil worn down to near uselessness.
An old prayer bead, loose from whatever strand it belonged to.
A slip of paper with someone's name and an address.
A handful of mixed nuts and dried fruit, loose in the pocket.
A small piece of polished glass, smooth-edged, possibly from a broken bottle.
A signet ring belonging to a family the owner does not appear to be related to.
A small paper packet of salt.
A leather cord with a pendant removed but the clasp still closed.
A tooth — human, adult, pulled cleanly.
A small pouch of sand with a label in a different language.
A note that appears to be a draft apology, crossed out three times.
A short length of thin wire, coiled.
A used match, still smelling faintly of sulfur.
A small scrap of leather with markings that might be initials.
A few grains of an unusual dark seed.
A playing card — not from a standard deck.
A cork stopper from a small bottle.
A promissory note for a modest sum, signed but undated.
A tiny brass key with no apparent lock.
A small ball of twine.
A piece of charcoal wrapped in paper to keep it from staining.
A small stone with a natural hole through it, worn smooth.
A handful of small pebbles — white, same size, as though collected deliberately.
A stub of sealing wax, red.
A folded piece of cloth, small, that appears to have been part of something larger.
Two small silver coins and one large copper one.
A thumb-sized wooden figure, crudely carved.
A piece of paper with numbers arranged in a column that may be a code or a budget.
A child's marble, glass, with a blue swirl inside.
A small jar of boot polish, nearly empty.
A scrap of fabric with a partial embroidery — flowers, half finished.
A small metal compass that no longer points reliably.
An invoice stamped PAID and then also DISPUTED.
A small carved animal — a rabbit, or possibly a dog depending on the angle.
A ticket stub from a theater performance.
A single glove, left hand, high quality.
A small jar of preserved jam, sealed with wax, labeled in a child's handwriting.
A note reading 'Second door on the left' with no other context.
A coin from a distant country, unfamiliar denomination.
A twist of paper containing a pinch of dried mushroom.
A scrap of paper with a single word on it that appears to be a name.
A small bronze medal from an event or competition, with a ribbon that has been cut.
A lock of white hair tied with blue thread.
A pair of folded spectacles with one cracked lens.
A packet of dried flowers with a smell that is immediately familiar and uncategorizable.
A small pot of ink, corked, about two-thirds full.
A note in coded writing.
A folded paper with a recipe on it, annotated in the margins by a different hand.
Three identical iron tokens stamped with a specific number.
A child's tooth wrapped in a scrap of cloth.
A small roll of paper that, when unrolled, contains a pressed insect.
A whetstone, palm-sized, well-used.
A bundle of six matches and no striker.
A broken seal — wax, red, from a letter long since opened.
A small mirror, the size of a palm, slightly warped.
A piece of string tied into a shape that might be intentional.
A scrap of paper with a sketch of a face. Unfinished.
A small empty bottle that still smells strongly of something.
A note with a time and a location and nothing else.
A short candle on a small tin dish, dribbled with old wax.
A worn token commemorating a military campaign from two decades ago.
A small roll of cheesecloth containing a lump of something soft and pungent.
A crumpled letter that has been read so many times it's falling apart at the folds.
A smooth pebble with a name scratched into it.
A set of four small dice, mismatched sizes.
A dried sprig of something that smells like lavender but isn't lavender.
A small needle and length of thread, wrapped around a card.
A worn copper ring, bent slightly out of round.
A ticket for a ferry crossing that has already been used, stamped twice.
A small flat piece of stone with a rune scratched into it.
A pair of wool ear coverings, rolled into a ball.
Nothing. The pocket has a hole in the bottom. Whatever was there is gone.
